channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: [8] Spiral spin liquid resilient to quantization in the frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet GdZnPO
item.description:
Last authors: Yi Cui, Yuesheng Li

Authors: Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of spin liquid in frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet.

Tags: #spinliquid #antiferromagnet #honeycomb

Abstract: Author(s): Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, Haijun Liao, Weiqiang Yu, Yi Cui, and Yuesheng Li Spin liquids usually emerge in low-spin ( S =½ or 1) systems with strong quantum fluctuations. Here, the authors show that GdZnPO—a structurally disorder-free, high-spin ( S =7⁄2) frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet—defies this expectation. Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) measurements reveal persistent spin dynamics and spin-liquid behavior down to the lowest accessible temperatures in GdZnPO. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174402] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, Haijun Liao, Weiqiang Yu, Yi Cui, and Yuesheng Li

Spin liquids usually emerge in low-spin (S=½ or 1) systems with strong quantum fluctuations. Here, the authors show that GdZnPO—a structurally disorder-free, high-spin (S=7⁄2) frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet—defies this expectation. Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) measurements reveal persistent spin dynamics and spin-liquid behavior down to the lowest accessible temperatures in GdZnPO.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174402] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Spiral spin liquid resilient to quantization in the frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet GdZnPO
item.dc:creator: Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Pressure-induced spin reorientation and isostructural phase transition in Sm0.5Y0.5FeO3
item.description:
Last authors: Hongliang Dong, Bin Chen

Authors: Chao Qian, Wenming Qi, Jun Deng, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of spin reorientation and phase transition in a strongly correlated orthoferrite.

Tags: #correlatedmaterials #ferromagnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Chao Qian, Wenming Qi, Jun Deng, Xuefeng Zhou, Zhikai Zhu, Yi Yang, Zihua Wu, Yiming Cao, Dayong Tan, Anwar Hushur, Qingyang Hu, Min Gao, Hongliang Dong, and Bin Chen The rare-earth orthoferrite family is prototypical strongly correlated systems, known for its highly tunable ferromagnetism properties. Among these materials, $\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{m}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Y}}_{0.5}\mathrm{Fe}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$ exhibits potential for applications becaue of its near room tempe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184104] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chao Qian, Wenming Qi, Jun Deng, Xuefeng Zhou, Zhikai Zhu, Yi Yang, Zihua Wu, Yiming Cao, Dayong Tan, Anwar Hushur, Qingyang Hu, Min Gao, Hongliang Dong, and Bin Chen

The rare-earth orthoferrite family is prototypical strongly correlated systems, known for its highly tunable ferromagnetism properties. Among these materials, Sm0.5Y0.5FeO3 exhibits potential for applications becaue of its near room temperature spin reorientation transition. However, a comprehensive…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184104] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Pressure-induced spin reorientation and isostructural phase transition in Sm0.5Y0.5FeO3
item.dc:creator: Chao Qian, Wenming Qi, Jun Deng, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Structure, structural phase transitions, mechanical properties, defects

item.title: [9] Mechanism of anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in quasi-two-dimensional MnAlGe epitaxial thin films
item.description:
Last authors: Taisuke T. Sasaki, Yuya Sakuraba

Authors: Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Comprehensive study of anomalous Hall effect and Berry curvature in a quasi-2D magnetic material.

Tags: #anomalousHall #Berrycurvature #quasi-2Dmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, Benugopal Bairagya, Keisuke Masuda, Weinan Zhou, Taisuke T. Sasaki, and Yuya Sakuraba We report a comprehensive experimental and theoretical study of the anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in (001)-oriented epitaxial MnAlGe thin films. First-principles calculations of intrinsic anomalous Hall conductivity originating from Berry curvature $({σ}_{xy}^{int})$ predict a large value, $∼856… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, Benugopal Bairagya, Keisuke Masuda, Weinan Zhou, Taisuke T. Sasaki, and Yuya Sakuraba

We report a comprehensive experimental and theoretical study of the anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in (001)-oriented epitaxial MnAlGe thin films. First-principles calculations of intrinsic anomalous Hall conductivity originating from Berry curvature (σxyint) predict a large value, ∼856Scm−1, at 0…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Mechanism of anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in quasi-two-dimensional MnAlGe epitaxial thin films
item.dc:creator: Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Competing magnetic phases in Cr3+δTe4 are spatially segregated
item.description:
Last authors: Samaresh Guchhait, J. M. Tranquada

Authors: V. K. Bhartiya, Anirban Goswami, Nicholas Ng, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of competing magnetic and tunable topological phases in a vdW ferromagnet.

Tags: #topological #vanderWaals #ferromagnetism

Abstract: Author(s): V. K. Bhartiya, Anirban Goswami, Nicholas Ng, Wei Tian, Matthew G. Tucker, Niraj Aryal, Lijun Wu, Weiguo Yin, Yimei Zhu, Milinda Abeykoon, Emmanuel Yakubu, Samaresh Guchhait, and J. M. Tranquada ${\mathrm{Cr}}_{1+x}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}$ is a self-intercalated van der Waals system that is of current interest for its room-temperature room-temperature ferromagnetic (FM) phases and tunable topological properties. In bulk samples, the strain from the interstitial Cr ions leads to distinct structura… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184406] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): V. K. Bhartiya, Anirban Goswami, Nicholas Ng, Wei Tian, Matthew G. Tucker, Niraj Aryal, Lijun Wu, Weiguo Yin, Yimei Zhu, Milinda Abeykoon, Emmanuel Yakubu, Samaresh Guchhait, and J. M. Tranquada

Cr1+xTe2 is a self-intercalated van der Waals system that is of current interest for its room-temperature room-temperature ferromagnetic (FM) phases and tunable topological properties. In bulk samples, the strain from the interstitial Cr ions leads to distinct structural phases for different ranges …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184406] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Competing magnetic phases in Cr3+δTe4 are spatially segregated
item.dc:creator: V. K. Bhartiya, Anirban Goswami, Nicholas Ng, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [9] Dual-switch control of a layer-locked anomalous valley Hall effect in a sliding ferroelectric antiferromagnet
item.description:
Last authors: Jiansheng Dong, Jianing Tan

Authors: Quan Shen, Wenhu Liao, Degao Xu, Jiansheng Dong, Jianing Tan

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on dual-switch control of anomalous valley Hall effect in 2D ferroelectric antiferromagnets.

Tags: #anomalousHall #antiferromagnetism #2Dmaterials #valleytronics

Abstract: Author(s): Quan Shen, Wenhu Liao, Degao Xu, Jiansheng Dong, and Jianing Tan The integration of ferroelectric (FE) and antiferromagnetic (AFM) orders in two-dimensional (2D) materials provides a promising avenue for the nonvolatile control of coupled spin and valley degrees of freedom, a capability central to advancing spin-valleytronics. However, realizing a single material… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184407] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Quan Shen, Wenhu Liao, Degao Xu, Jiansheng Dong, and Jianing Tan

The integration of ferroelectric (FE) and antiferromagnetic (AFM) orders in two-dimensional (2D) materials provides a promising avenue for the nonvolatile control of coupled spin and valley degrees of freedom, a capability central to advancing spin-valleytronics. However, realizing a single material…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184407] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Dual-switch control of a layer-locked anomalous valley Hall effect in a sliding ferroelectric antiferromagnet
item.dc:creator: Quan Shen, Wenhu Liao, Degao Xu, Jiansheng Dong, Jianing Tan

item.title: [7] Temperature-dependent optical and magneto-optical spectra of ferromagnetic bcc Fe
item.description:
Last authors: David G. Cahill, André Schleife

Authors: Kisung Kang, David G. Cahill, André Schleife

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Temperature-dependent optical and magneto-optical spectra of ferromagnetic Fe.

Tags: #magneto-optical #ferromagnetism #spectroscopy

Abstract: Author(s): Kisung Kang, David G. Cahill, and André Schleife Optical and magneto-optical properties of magnetic materials have been widely exploited to characterize magnetic structures and phenomena; however, their temperature dependence is not well understood. This study implements the supercell approach with thermal lattice and magnetic disorders to obtain … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184408] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kisung Kang, David G. Cahill, and André Schleife

Optical and magneto-optical properties of magnetic materials have been widely exploited to characterize magnetic structures and phenomena; however, their temperature dependence is not well understood. This study implements the supercell approach with thermal lattice and magnetic disorders to obtain …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184408] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Temperature-dependent optical and magneto-optical spectra of ferromagnetic bcc Fe

item.title: [7] Prediction of two-dimensional Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya magnetism
item.description:
Last authors: Xiangping Jiang, Jun-Ming Liu

Authors: Xiaokun Huang, Yunying Mo, Liang Liu, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical prediction of Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya magnetism in 2D intrinsic magnetic materials.

Tags: #2Dmaterials #magnetism #DMI

Abstract: Author(s): Xiaokun Huang, Yunying Mo, Liang Liu, Bingwen Zhang, Guang Song, Qianli Yang, Chao Chen, Xiangping Jiang, and Jun-Ming Liu In the emerging field of two-dimensional (2D) intrinsic magnetic materials, the Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) has attracted wide attention for its crucial role in triggering exotic spin textures, provided that the Heisenberg exchange supplies the long-range magnetic order as a background. … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184409] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaokun Huang, Yunying Mo, Liang Liu, Bingwen Zhang, Guang Song, Qianli Yang, Chao Chen, Xiangping Jiang, and Jun-Ming Liu

In the emerging field of two-dimensional (2D) intrinsic magnetic materials, the Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) has attracted wide attention for its crucial role in triggering exotic spin textures, provided that the Heisenberg exchange supplies the long-range magnetic order as a background. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184409] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Prediction of two-dimensional Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya magnetism

item.title: [8] Tunable magnetic excitations in the honeycomb antiferromagnet (Co,Ni)TiO3
item.description:
Last authors: Stuart Calder, Despina Louca

Authors: Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, Despina Louca

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of magnetic excitations in a honeycomb antiferromagnet using neutron scattering.

Tags: #antiferromagnetism #honeycomb #magneticexcitations

Abstract: Author(s): Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, and Despina Louca The solid solution of the honeycomb antiferromagnet (AFM) ${\mathrm{Co}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{TiO}}_{3}$ (CNTO) was synthesized by mixing a 1:1 molar ratio of ${\mathrm{CoTiO}}_{3}$ (CTO) and ${\mathrm{NiTiO}}_{3}$ (NTO). Powder neutron scattering was used to determine the structure and … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184412] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, and Despina Louca

The solid solution of the honeycomb antiferromagnet (AFM) Co0.5Ni0.5TiO3 (CNTO) was synthesized by mixing a 1:1 molar ratio of CoTiO3 (CTO) and NiTiO3 (NTO). Powder neutron scattering was used to determine the structure and magnetic spectrum, where the nuclear and magnetic structures resemble those …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184412] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Tunable magnetic excitations in the honeycomb antiferromagnet (Co,Ni)TiO3
item.dc:creator: Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, Despina Louca
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [9] Theoretical design of the large topological magnetoelectric effect in the Co-intercalated NbS2 structure
item.description:
Last authors: Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical design of large topological magnetoelectric and anomalous Hall effect in Co-intercalated NbS2.

Tags: #topological #anomalousHall #magnetoelectric #NbS2 #2Dmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Hyowon Park and Ivar Martin A triangular Co-ion lattice intercalated between 1-H ${\mathrm{NbS}}_{2}$ layers can exhibit a large anomalous Hall effect (AHE) due to the finite scalar spin chirality originating from the noncoplanar $3q$ ordering of Co spins. This large AHE occurs when the scalar spin chirality is uniform in all … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184413] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hyowon Park and Ivar Martin

A triangular Co-ion lattice intercalated between 1-H NbS2 layers can exhibit a large anomalous Hall effect (AHE) due to the finite scalar spin chirality originating from the noncoplanar 3q ordering of Co spins. This large AHE occurs when the scalar spin chirality is uniform in all Co layers, as inde…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184413] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Theoretical design of the large topological magnetoelectric effect in the Co-intercalated NbS2 structure
item.dc:creator: Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Equivalence class of emergent single Weyl fermion lattice models in three dimensions: Gapless superconductors and superfluids versus chiral fermions
item.description:
Last authors: Gabriel Meyniel, Fei Zhou

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on Weyl fermion lattice models, directly relevant to user's strong interest in Weyl semimetals.

Tags: #Weylsemimetals #theory

Abstract: Author(s): Gabriel Meyniel and Fei Zhou In this article, we put forward a practical but generic approach towards constructing a large family of $(3+1)$-dimensional (3D) lattice models which can naturally lead to a single Weyl cone in the infrared limit. Our proposal relies on spontaneous charge $\text{U}(1)$ symmetry breaking via a pair c… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184502] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabriel Meyniel and Fei Zhou

In this article, we put forward a practical but generic approach towards constructing a large family of (3+1)-dimensional (3D) lattice models which can naturally lead to a single Weyl cone in the infrared limit. Our proposal relies on spontaneous charge U(1) symmetry breaking via a pair condensate t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184502] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Equivalence class of emergent single Weyl fermion lattice models in three dimensions: Gapless superconductors and superfluids versus chiral fermions
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Meyniel, Fei Zhou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [7] Unconventional superconductivity in La3Ni2O7 from the perspective of symmetry
item.description:
Last authors: Jun Quan, Yusheng Hou

Authors: Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, Yusheng Hou

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on unconventional superconductivity in an important new quantum material, La3Ni2O7.

Tags: #superconductivity #nickelates

Abstract: Author(s): Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, and Yusheng Hou The recently discovered superconductor ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$ has attracted significant attention due to its remarkably high transition temperature (${T}_{c}$) under high pressure. Shortly after this discovery, thin-film ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184505] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, and Yusheng Hou

The recently discovered superconductor La3Ni2O7 has attracted significant attention due to its remarkably high transition temperature (Tc) under high pressure. Shortly after this discovery, thin-film La3Ni2O7 was demonstrated to exhibit ambient-pressure superconductivity; however, the corresponding …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184505] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Unconventional superconductivity in La3Ni2O7 from the perspective of symmetry
item.dc:creator: Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, Yusheng Hou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [7] Superconductivity in UTe2 from local noncentrosymmetricity
item.description:
Last authors: Ryuji Hakuno, Youichi Yanase

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on an important spin-triplet superconductor candidate UTe2, relevant quantum material.

Tags: #superconductivity #heavyfermion

Abstract: Author(s): Ryuji Hakuno and Youichi Yanase Superconductivity in ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$ has attracted significant attention, as it is widely recognized as a promising candidate for a spin-triplet superconductor. However, the symmetry of superconductivity and the microscopic origin of spin-triplet pairing remain subjects of debate. Nevertheless,… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195107] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ryuji Hakuno and Youichi Yanase

Superconductivity in UTe2 has attracted significant attention, as it is widely recognized as a promising candidate for a spin-triplet superconductor. However, the symmetry of superconductivity and the microscopic origin of spin-triplet pairing remain subjects of debate. Nevertheless, various experim…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195107] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Superconductivity in UTe2 from local noncentrosymmetricity
item.dc:creator: Ryuji Hakuno, Youichi Yanase

item.title: [9] Chiral edge plasmons in quantum anomalous Hall insulators
item.description:
Last authors: Jianhui Zhou, Yugui Yao

Authors: Furu Zhang, Chenxi Ding, Jianhui Zhou, Yugui Yao

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Directly relevant to Berry curvature, anomalous Hall, and topological quantum materials.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #anomalousHall #topological #plasmons

Abstract: Author(s): Furu Zhang, Chenxi Ding, Jianhui Zhou, and Yugui Yao We find that the Berry curvature splits the edge plasmons propagating along the opposite directions in quantum anomalous Hall insulators even with vanishing Chern number. When the bulk is insulating, a single unidirectional edge plasmon survives and becomes acoustic in the long-wavelength limit, whi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Furu Zhang, Chenxi Ding, Jianhui Zhou, and Yugui Yao

We find that the Berry curvature splits the edge plasmons propagating along the opposite directions in quantum anomalous Hall insulators even with vanishing Chern number. When the bulk is insulating, a single unidirectional edge plasmon survives and becomes acoustic in the long-wavelength limit, whi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Chiral edge plasmons in quantum anomalous Hall insulators
item.dc:creator: Furu Zhang, Chenxi Ding, Jianhui Zhou, Yugui Yao

item.title: [8] Non-Fermi-liquid behavior and Fermi-surface expansion induced by van Hove driven ferromagnetic fluctuations: Dual triply irreducible local expansion analysis
item.description:
Last authors: Andrey A. Katanin, Evgeny A. Stepanov

Authors: Ilia S. Dedov, Andrey A. Katanin, Evgeny A. Stepanov

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on non-Fermi-liquid behavior and Fermi surface, directly relevant for ARPES and electronic structure.

Tags: #Fermisurface #non-Fermiliquid #vanHovesingularity #ferromagnetism #theory

Abstract: Author(s): Ilia S. Dedov, Andrey A. Katanin, and Evgeny A. Stepanov We consider the electronic and magnetic properties of the Hubbard model on a square lattice with the Fermi level near van Hove singularity and the ratio of the next-nearest-neighbor and nearest-neighbor hoppings ${t}^{′}/t=−0.45$, which favors the ferromagnetic instability. We find that a self-consi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205109] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ilia S. Dedov, Andrey A. Katanin, and Evgeny A. Stepanov

We consider the electronic and magnetic properties of the Hubbard model on a square lattice with the Fermi level near van Hove singularity and the ratio of the next-nearest-neighbor and nearest-neighbor hoppings t′/t=−0.45, which favors the ferromagnetic instability. We find that a self-consistent c…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205109] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Non-Fermi-liquid behavior and Fermi-surface expansion induced by van Hove driven ferromagnetic fluctuations: Dual triply irreducible local expansion analysis
item.dc:creator: Ilia S. Dedov, Andrey A. Katanin, Evgeny A. Stepanov

item.title: [7] Origin of diverse interlayer charge redistribution in transition metal dichalcogenides
item.description:
Last authors: Jiang-Long Wang, Xing-Qiang Shi

Authors: Yu-Meng Gao, Nie-Wei Wang, Shi-Xuan Yuan, Wen-Xin Xia, Jiang-Long Wang, Xing-Qiang Shi

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant for electronic structure and charge distribution in layered TMD quantum materials.

Tags: #TMD #electronicstructure #layeredmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Yu-Meng Gao, Nie-Wei Wang, Shi-Xuan Yuan, Wen-Xin Xia, Jiang-Long Wang, and Xing-Qiang Shi The interlayer quasi-chemical-bonding (QCB) interactions of two-dimensional layered materials advance the research field of interlayer engineering and cause interlayer charge density redistributions (ICDRs). The ICDRs have been reported experimentally and theoretically, showing different redistribut… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205302] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu-Meng Gao, Nie-Wei Wang, Shi-Xuan Yuan, Wen-Xin Xia, Jiang-Long Wang, and Xing-Qiang Shi

The interlayer quasi-chemical-bonding (QCB) interactions of two-dimensional layered materials advance the research field of interlayer engineering and cause interlayer charge density redistributions (ICDRs). The ICDRs have been reported experimentally and theoretically, showing different redistribut…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205302] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Origin of diverse interlayer charge redistribution in transition metal dichalcogenides
item.dc:creator: Yu-Meng Gao, Nie-Wei Wang, Shi-Xuan Yuan, Wen-Xin Xia, Jiang-Long Wang, Xing-Qiang Shi

item.title: [7] Twisted bilayer graphene as a terahertz plasmonic crystal
item.description:
Last authors: Brian S. Vermilyea, Michael M. Fogler

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of topological states in twisted bilayer graphene, a relevant quantum material platform.

Tags: #graphene #twisted #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Brian S. Vermilyea and Michael M. Fogler We study surface plasmons in minimally twisted gapped bilayer graphene that contains a triangular network of partial dislocations (or AB-BA domain walls) hosting topologically protected one-dimensional electronic states. We show that this system behaves as a plasmonic crystal and we calculate its ba… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205406] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Brian S. Vermilyea and Michael M. Fogler

We study surface plasmons in minimally twisted gapped bilayer graphene that contains a triangular network of partial dislocations (or AB-BA domain walls) hosting topologically protected one-dimensional electronic states. We show that this system behaves as a plasmonic crystal and we calculate its ba…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205406] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Twisted bilayer graphene as a terahertz plasmonic crystal
item.dc:creator: Brian S. Vermilyea, Michael M. Fogler

item.title: [7] Dynamical moment fragmentation in the all-in all-out pyrochlore Nd2Sn2O7
item.description:
Last authors: Joseph W. Kolis, Adam A. Aczel

Authors: Yi Luo, Matthew S. Powell, Joseph A. M. Paddison, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of magnetism and flatband in a pyrochlore, relevant for quantum materials with complex magnetic order.

Tags: #magnetism #flatband #neutronscattering

Abstract: Author(s): Yi Luo, Matthew S. Powell, Joseph A. M. Paddison, Brenden R. Ortiz, J. Ross Stewart, Joseph W. Kolis, and Adam A. Aczel We report single-crystal neutron spectroscopy and bulk characterization of hydrothermally grown ${\mathrm{Nd}}_{2}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$, revealing a dynamical moment fragmentation embedded within the all-in all-out ordered state. The spectra show a nearly flat band with pinch-point-like… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L180402] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yi Luo, Matthew S. Powell, Joseph A. M. Paddison, Brenden R. Ortiz, J. Ross Stewart, Joseph W. Kolis, and Adam A. Aczel

We report single-crystal neutron spectroscopy and bulk characterization of hydrothermally grown Nd2Sn2O7, revealing a dynamical moment fragmentation embedded within the all-in all-out ordered state. The spectra show a nearly flat band with pinch-point-like momentum dependence, accompanied by dispers…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L180402] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Dynamical moment fragmentation in the all-in all-out pyrochlore Nd2Sn2O7
item.dc:creator: Yi Luo, Matthew S. Powell, Joseph A. M. Paddison, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Engineering subgap states in superconductors by the symmetry of altermagnetism
item.description:
Last authors: Yukio Tanaka, Yuri Fukaya

Authors: Bo Lu, Phillip Mercebach, Pablo Burset, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on altermagnetism and superconductivity with potential for experimental detection.

Tags: #altermagnetism #superconductivity #STM/STS

Abstract: Author(s): Bo Lu, Phillip Mercebach, Pablo Burset, Keiji Yada, Jorge Cayao, Yukio Tanaka, and Yuri Fukaya The authors demonstrate here that altermagnetism is a powerful phenomenon for realizing highly tunable subgap states in unconventional superconductors at zero net magnetization. In particular, they unveil bulk and surface subgap states exhibiting distinct highly tunable dispersions, which are entirely controlled by altermagnetic symmetries. These subgap states are further shown to induce direct measurable conductance signals, thereby offering a way to detect altermagnetism and superconductivity. These results establish altermagnetism as a versatile mechanism for functionalizing superconducting devices. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L180501] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bo Lu, Phillip Mercebach, Pablo Burset, Keiji Yada, Jorge Cayao, Yukio Tanaka, and Yuri Fukaya

The authors demonstrate here that altermagnetism is a powerful phenomenon for realizing highly tunable subgap states in unconventional superconductors at zero net magnetization. In particular, they unveil bulk and surface subgap states exhibiting distinct highly tunable dispersions, which are entirely controlled by altermagnetic symmetries. These subgap states are further shown to induce direct measurable conductance signals, thereby offering a way to detect altermagnetism and superconductivity. These results establish altermagnetism as a versatile mechanism for functionalizing superconducting devices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L180501] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Engineering subgap states in superconductors by the symmetry of altermagnetism
item.dc:creator: Bo Lu, Phillip Mercebach, Pablo Burset, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [8] Quantum geometric origin of the intrinsic nonlinear Hall effect
item.description:
Last authors: Laura Classen, Andreas P. Schnyder

Authors: Yannis Ulrich, Johannes Mitscherling, Laura Classen, Andreas P. Schnyder

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Connects quantum geometry, Berry curvature, and topological band crossings to nonlinear Hall effect.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #anomalousHall #topologicalsemimetals #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Yannis Ulrich, Johannes Mitscherling, Laura Classen, and Andreas P. Schnyder Quantum geometry, which encodes the momentum-space structure of Bloch electrons in crystals, both governs nonlinear transport and can be probed through it. Using a systematic, gauge-invariant projector formalism, the authors develop here a quantum-geometric classification of the nonlinear Hall effect. They identify a previously overlooked intraband quantum metric dipole, disentangle contributions by symmetry, and clarify inconsistencies in earlier formulations. The work highlights nonlinear transport as a sensitive probe of quantum geometry, especially near topological band crossings. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201107] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yannis Ulrich, Johannes Mitscherling, Laura Classen, and Andreas P. Schnyder

Quantum geometry, which encodes the momentum-space structure of Bloch electrons in crystals, both governs nonlinear transport and can be probed through it. Using a systematic, gauge-invariant projector formalism, the authors develop here a quantum-geometric classification of the nonlinear Hall effect. They identify a previously overlooked intraband quantum metric dipole, disentangle contributions by symmetry, and clarify inconsistencies in earlier formulations. The work highlights nonlinear transport as a sensitive probe of quantum geometry, especially near topological band crossings.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L201107] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Quantum geometric origin of the intrinsic nonlinear Hall effect
item.dc:creator: Yannis Ulrich, Johannes Mitscherling, Laura Classen, Andreas P. Schnyder
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: [7] Sliding-driven spin-polarized transport of magnon orbital moments in an altermagnet
item.description:
Last authors: Fawei Zheng, Yugui Yao

Authors: Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, Yugui Yao

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Investigates spin-polarized transport of magnon orbital moments in altermagnets, involving Berry curvature.

Tags: #altermagnetism #Berrycurvature #transport #magneticmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, and Yugui Yao Here, we propose a method to introduce the magnon orbital Nernst effect in twisted collinear magnets through broken spatial inversion symmetry. We demonstrate that nonzero Berry curvature and orbital moments of magnons can be achieved by atomic translation, including spin-polarized transport of magn… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174401] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, and Yugui Yao

Here, we propose a method to introduce the magnon orbital Nernst effect in twisted collinear magnets through broken spatial inversion symmetry. We demonstrate that nonzero Berry curvature and orbital moments of magnons can be achieved by atomic translation, including spin-polarized transport of magn…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174401] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Sliding-driven spin-polarized transport of magnon orbital moments in an altermagnet
item.dc:creator: Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, Yugui Yao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Helical phases and Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces probed by superconducting diode effects
item.description:
Last authors: Jaglul Hasan, Alex Levchenko

Authors: Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, Alex Levchenko

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Discusses Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces in superconductors with a modern experimental probe.

Tags: #Fermisurface #superconductivity #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, and Alex Levchenko Noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NCSs) with Rashba spin-orbit coupling (SOC) and in-plane magnetic fields have emerged as natural platforms for realizing both the bulk superconducting diode effect (SDE) and the Josephson diode effect (JDE)—phenomena characterized by unequal critical currents in o… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174501] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, and Alex Levchenko

Noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NCSs) with Rashba spin-orbit coupling (SOC) and in-plane magnetic fields have emerged as natural platforms for realizing both the bulk superconducting diode effect (SDE) and the Josephson diode effect (JDE)—phenomena characterized by unequal critical currents in o…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174501] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Helical phases and Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces probed by superconducting diode effects
item.dc:creator: Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, Alex Levchenko

item.title: [10] Josephson spectroscopy study of kagome superconductors toward the deep point-contact regime
item.description:
Last authors: Yuanyuan Zhao, Jia-Xin Yin

Authors: Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, et al.

Score 10/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct hit: Josephson STM study of kagome superconductors, probing the order parameter.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #kagome #superconductivity #STM/STS

Abstract: Author(s): Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, Mu-Wei Gao, Guowei Liu, Yuanyuan Zhao, and Jia-Xin Yin Josephson scanning tunneling microscopy (JSTM) has emerged as an important technique for probing the superconducting order parameter at the atomic scale. However, the Josephson current in JSTM may behave quite differently when the coupling strength varies. Here, we push the junction to the deep poin… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174502] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, Mu-Wei Gao, Guowei Liu, Yuanyuan Zhao, and Jia-Xin Yin

Josephson scanning tunneling microscopy (JSTM) has emerged as an important technique for probing the superconducting order parameter at the atomic scale. However, the Josephson current in JSTM may behave quite differently when the coupling strength varies. Here, we push the junction to the deep poin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174502] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [10] Josephson spectroscopy study of kagome superconductors toward the deep point-contact regime

item.title: [7] Probing the chirality of trigonal selenium and tellurium by spin and orbital Hall effects
item.description:
Last authors: Wei Ren, Heng Gao

Authors: Yuting Xiong, Yingjie Hu, Wei Ren, Heng Gao

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on probing chirality in specific materials via spin/orbital Hall effects, relevant for transport.

Tags: #anomalousHall #transport #topologicalsemimetals

Abstract: Author(s): Yuting Xiong, Yingjie Hu, Wei Ren, and Heng Gao Chiral crystals exhibit enantiomer-dependent transport phenomena that generate pure spin or orbital currents, while the handedness sensitivity of spin and orbital Hall conductivities (SHC/OHC) remains insufficiently understood. Using first-principles calculations, we demonstrate that trigonal seleni… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184402] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuting Xiong, Yingjie Hu, Wei Ren, and Heng Gao

Chiral crystals exhibit enantiomer-dependent transport phenomena that generate pure spin or orbital currents, while the handedness sensitivity of spin and orbital Hall conductivities (SHC/OHC) remains insufficiently understood. Using first-principles calculations, we demonstrate that trigonal seleni…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184402] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Probing the chirality of trigonal selenium and tellurium by spin and orbital Hall effects

item.title: [7] Slow-phonon control of spin Edelstein effect in Rashba d-wave altermagnets
item.description:
Last authors: Jacob Linder, James K. Freericks

Authors: Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, James K. Freericks

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on phonon-controlled spin transport effects in altermagnets with spin-split bands.

Tags: #altermagnetism #transport #electronicstructure

Abstract: Author(s): Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, and James K. Freericks Altermagnets have zero net magnetization yet feature spin-split bands. Here, we investigate how slow lattice vibrations (phonons) influence both the intrinsic and externally induced spin polarizations in two-dimensional $d$-wave altermagnets. For the induced spin polarization, we employ a Rashba con… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184403] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, and James K. Freericks

Altermagnets have zero net magnetization yet feature spin-split bands. Here, we investigate how slow lattice vibrations (phonons) influence both the intrinsic and externally induced spin polarizations in two-dimensional d-wave altermagnets. For the induced spin polarization, we employ a Rashba conti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184403] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Slow-phonon control of spin Edelstein effect in Rashba d-wave altermagnets

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of type-II multiferroics in the context of altermagnetism.

Tags: #altermagnetism #magneticmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Songsong Yan, Hanjing Zhou, Dingwen Zhang, Huimei Liu, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wang Type-II multiferroics offer a promising route to strong magnetoelectric coupling due to the magnetic-order-driven ferroelectricity. Most experimentally realized type-II multiferroics exhibit noncollinear magnetic order, whose complex interaction with external fields can impede the practical control … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195105] Published Fri May 01, 2026

Author(s): Songsong Yan, Hanjing Zhou, Dingwen Zhang, Huimei Liu, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wang

Type-II multiferroics offer a promising route to strong magnetoelectric coupling due to the magnetic-order-driven ferroelectricity. Most experimentally realized type-II multiferroics exhibit noncollinear magnetic order, whose complex interaction with external fields can impede the practical control …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195105] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [7] Hund's metal physics in uranium mononitride
item.description:
Last authors: Mark R. Pederson, Eunja Kim

Authors: Byungkyun Kang, Stephen S. Micklo, Roy N. Herrera-Navarro, Mark R. Pederson, Eunja Kim

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on Hund's metal physics in a quantum material (UN), relevant for strong correlations.

Tags: #Kondo #stronglycorrelatedsystems #transport #heavyfermions

Abstract: Author(s): Byungkyun Kang, Stephen S. Micklo, Roy N. Herrera-Navarro, Mark R. Pederson, and Eunja Kim Uranium $5f$ electrons often yield heavy-fermion behavior via Kondo screening. However, the pronounced bad-metallic transport of uranium mononitride (UN) defies an incoherent Kondo explanation. Using density-functional theory combined with dynamical mean-field theory, we show that UN is a strongly c… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195106] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Byungkyun Kang, Stephen S. Micklo, Roy N. Herrera-Navarro, Mark R. Pederson, and Eunja Kim

Uranium 5f electrons often yield heavy-fermion behavior via Kondo screening. However, the pronounced bad-metallic transport of uranium mononitride (UN) defies an incoherent Kondo explanation. Using density-functional theory combined with dynamical mean-field theory, we show that UN is a strongly cor…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195106] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Hund's metal physics in uranium mononitride

Authors: Mounes Eslami, Amin Hakimi, Luis A. Jauregui, Filippo Capolino

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Connects Berry curvature dipole to chiral optical/transport effects in bulk tellurium.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #transport #topologicalsemimetals

Abstract: Author(s): Mounes Eslami, Amin Hakimi, Luis A. Jauregui, and Filippo Capolino We investigate the use of Berry curvature dipole in $n$-doped tellurium as a mechanism for achieving terahertz amplification and lasing by applying a DC electric field. When the electrical bias and wave vector are aligned along the trigonal $c$ axis, the right-handed circularly polarized mode experi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195202] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mounes Eslami, Amin Hakimi, Luis A. Jauregui, and Filippo Capolino

We investigate the use of Berry curvature dipole in n-doped tellurium as a mechanism for achieving terahertz amplification and lasing by applying a DC electric field. When the electrical bias and wave vector are aligned along the trigonal c axis, the right-handed circularly polarized mode experience…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195202] Published Fri May 01, 2026

Abstract: Author(s): Valery Ortiz Jimenez, Paul M. Haney, Farzad Mahfouzi, Ngoc Thanh Mai Tran, Albert F. Rigosi, and Curt A. Richter The quantum anomalous Hall effect shows great promise for the realization of the ohm without the need for an external magnetic field. The most mature material platform is magnetically doped topological insulators. In these materials, precise quantization is limited to low temperatures, with the acti… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195402] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Valery Ortiz Jimenez, Paul M. Haney, Farzad Mahfouzi, Ngoc Thanh Mai Tran, Albert F. Rigosi, and Curt A. Richter

The quantum anomalous Hall effect shows great promise for the realization of the ohm without the need for an external magnetic field. The most mature material platform is magnetically doped topological insulators. In these materials, precise quantization is limited to low temperatures, with the acti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195402] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [8] Optical signatures of ultrafast lattice distortion in the ternary type-II Weyl semimetal TaIrTe4
item.description:
Last authors: Jun Xiao, John C. Wright

Authors: David P. Lafayette, II, Li Cheng, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Investigation of a Weyl semimetal telluride relevant to user's interest in topological materials.

Tags: #Weyl #telluride #topology

Abstract: Author(s): David P. Lafayette, II, Li Cheng, Wenxu Duan, Jiangxu Li, Yangchen He, Yulu Mao, Ying Wang, Daniel A. Rhodes, Daniel D. Kohler, Yang Zhang, Jun Xiao, and John C. Wright ${T}_{d}$ phase type-II Weyl semimetals are of technological interest in part due to their novel sliding ferroelectricity and nontrivial topology being dependent on their van der Waals stacking orders. Optical manipulation of these stacking orders via interlayer sliding can lead to new types of ener… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205402] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): David P. Lafayette, II, Li Cheng, Wenxu Duan, Jiangxu Li, Yangchen He, Yulu Mao, Ying Wang, Daniel A. Rhodes, Daniel D. Kohler, Yang Zhang, Jun Xiao, and John C. Wright

Td phase type-II Weyl semimetals are of technological interest in part due to their novel sliding ferroelectricity and nontrivial topology being dependent on their van der Waals stacking orders. Optical manipulation of these stacking orders via interlayer sliding can lead to new types of energy-effi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205402] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [7] Large anomalous Hall effect in double-Q magnets without net scalar spin chirality
item.description:
Last authors: Satoru Ohgata, Satoru Hayami

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant study of anomalous Hall effect and complex magnetic orders in multi-Q systems.

Tags: #anomalousHall #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Satoru Ohgata and Satoru Hayami Multi-Q magnets consist of superposed spin-density waves with distinct magnetic modulation vectors, enabling a wide range of magnetic orders depending on their combination. Among them, topologically nontrivial spin textures, such as a magnetic skyrmion, have been extensively studied owing to the eme… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L180401] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Satoru Ohgata and Satoru Hayami

Multi-Q magnets consist of superposed spin-density waves with distinct magnetic modulation vectors, enabling a wide range of magnetic orders depending on their combination. Among them, topologically nontrivial spin textures, such as a magnetic skyrmion, have been extensively studied owing to the eme…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L180401] Published Fri May 01, 2026

Shen, Emilia Morosan, and Douglas Natelson Mesoscopic transport measurements are underexplored as probes of quasiparticles and their properties in correlated metals. The mixed valence compound ${\mathrm{YbAl}}_{3}$ exhibits a single-ion Kondo temperature of 670 K, while thermodynamic and transport properties (probed with specific heat, magne… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201101] Published Fri May 01, 2026

Mesoscopic transport measurements are underexplored as probes of quasiparticles and their properties in correlated metals. The mixed valence compound YbAl3 exhibits a single-ion Kondo temperature of 670 K, while thermodynamic and transport properties (probed with specific heat, magnetic susceptibili…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L201101] Published Fri May 01, 2026

Last authors: Michael Merz, Mingquan He

Authors: Jingjing Jing, Andreas Eich, Yiqiu Liu, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental characterization of a new kagome lattice material, highly relevant to user's projects.

Tags: #kagome #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Jingjing Jing, Andreas Eich, Yiqiu Liu, Liran Wang, Lunhua He, Aifeng Wang, Yisheng Chai, Young Sun, Yi Cui, Frederic Hardy, Christoph Meingast, Weiqiang Yu, Xinrun Mi, Michael Merz, and Mingquan He The square-kagome lattice (SKL) antiferromagnet, composed of two-dimensional corner-sharing triangles, provides a prominent platform for studying frustrated magnetism. However, material realizations of the SKL remain scarce. Here, we report the single-crystal growth, structural characterization, mag… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 134447] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jingjing Jing, Andreas Eich, Yiqiu Liu, Liran Wang, Lunhua He, Aifeng Wang, Yisheng Chai, Young Sun, Yi Cui, Frederic Hardy, Christoph Meingast, Weiqiang Yu, Xinrun Mi, Michael Merz, and Mingquan He

The square-kagome lattice (SKL) antiferromagnet, composed of two-dimensional corner-sharing triangles, provides a prominent platform for studying frustrated magnetism. However, material realizations of the SKL remain scarce. Here, we report the single-crystal growth, structural characterization, mag…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134447] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026

item.title: [7] Topology driven magnetic memory of OsB2 surface states
item.description:
Last authors: Bahadur Singh, Goutam Sheet

Authors: Nikhlesh S. Mehta, Bikash Patra, Mona Garg, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Exploration of topological surface states and superconductivity, relevant to spectroscopic interests.

Tags: #topology #surfacestates #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Nikhlesh S. Mehta, Bikash Patra, Mona Garg, Ghulam Mohmad, Pooja Bhardwaj, Savita Chaudhary, Jaskaran Singh, Yogesh Singh, Bahadur Singh, and Goutam Sheet The development of superconducting data storage devices is one of the coveted goals for next-generation low-power electronics, and interaction between superconducting and nontrivial topological states can potentially be useful to achieve this goal. Here we show that the superconducting diboride ${\m… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 134529] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026

Author(s): Nikhlesh S. Mehta, Bikash Patra, Mona Garg, Ghulam Mohmad, Pooja Bhardwaj, Savita Chaudhary, Jaskaran Singh, Yogesh Singh, Bahadur Singh, and Goutam Sheet

The development of superconducting data storage devices is one of the coveted goals for next-generation low-power electronics, and interaction between superconducting and nontrivial topological states can potentially be useful to achieve this goal. Here we show that the superconducting diboride OsB2…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134529] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
